narrative: OMX30 opens with tailwinds from continental Europe, particularly Germany's +1.45% surge which typically correlates strongly with Swedish industrials. The weighted correlation signal of +0.517% leans constructive. However, technicals show caution: RSI at 39.66 is neutral-to-oversold, MACD below signal with negative histogram (-7.426), and ADX at 22.2 suggests moderate momentum with bearish DI bias. The index sits 6.5% above its 200-MA but only +0.1% above the 50-MA, indicating consolidation. USD/SEK at 9.323 (+0.33%) is constructive for exporters. Key risk: US 10Y yields at 4.481 compress tech valuations, and Shanghai weakness (-1.52%) signals Asia hesitation.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens +0.3% to +0.5% on European momentum carryover and USD strength. Watch 3180 (50-MA) as immediate resistance; support sits at 3085 (lower Bollinger band). Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo) should outperform on EUR strength and export tailwinds via USD/SEK move. Banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken) are net positive from higher yields (NIM expansion), but monitor rate sensitivity—10Y at 4.48% is a headwind for real estate (Balder, Castellum). Tech/Telecom (Ericsson, Hexagon) face headwind from NASDAQ flat and yield compression. Pharma/Defensive (AstraZeneca, Essity) may find bids on risk-off sentiment from UK political noise. Volume and intraday catalysts: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET.
drivers: Continental Europe strength (DAX +1.45%, CAC +0.93%) with high correlation (r=0.89, 0.85)
Bull market regime intact, +6.5% above 200-MA with +5.7% ROC-90 momentum
US 10Y yields elevated at 4.481% (+0.40%) — banks benefit but tech/growth pressured
Risk-off sentiment (UK political uncertainty) may support defensive rotation

sectorImpacts.reason: DAX +1.45% (high correlation 0.89) and USD/SEK +0.33% both support export-heavy engineering/machinery firms; tailwinds on foreign revenue conversion.
US 10Y yields +0.40% to 4.481% widens NIM expectations for Swedish banks; positive carry and lending margin environment.
Higher US and European yields (4.481%) increase refinancing costs and cap property valuations; mild headwind but not imminent stress.
NASDAQ +1.20% overnight is positive, but higher yields compress DCF valuations; mixed signals. Ericsson also exposed to China weakness (Shanghai -1.52%).
Gold +0.07% and copper -0.17% show mixed signals. Boliden stable but no strong directional push today.
UK political uncertainty and global risk-off sentiment favor defensive sectors; modest safe-haven bid.

watchList.reason: DAX +1.45% (peer sector), USD/SEK +0.33% (export benefit), and Industrials = 28% of OMX30. Good barometer for morning momentum.
NASDAQ +1.20% positive overnight, but Shanghai -1.52% is concerning (China exposure). Watch for divergence and earnings revisions.
Banks +18% of index. Higher yields at 4.481% directly expand NIM; likely to lead if indices hold bid.
Defensive rotation on UK political noise. Pharma typically rallies in risk-off; monitor for inflows.

keyLevels.strongResistance: 3230 (approx 52W range high reference, -5.4% from high implies ~3230-3240)

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic data today. US Jobless Claims (weekly) at 14:30 CET — monitor for labor market signals that could impact Fed rate expectations and SEK carry.
topRisk: MACD below signal and ADX showing moderate bearish DI bias suggests limited upside thrust; any negative US data at 14:30 CET could trigger quick reversal toward 3085. RSI at 39.66 is neutral but approaching oversold (30) — mean reversion risk on sharp down move.
technicalSummary: MACD below signal (histogram -7.426) — bearish divergence despite price strength
RSI 39.66: neutral, no overbought risk but watch for dip-buying opportunity if dips toward 30
Stochastic K=25.7, D=31.5: both neutral-to-oversold, no extremes
ADX 22.2 (moderate) with bearish DI bias: suggests weak directional conviction
Bollinger %B=0.22 (lower half): price coiling, narrow band (5.6%) indicates low volatility; breakout potential in either direction
ROC-90: +5.7% strong momentum supports continuation bias over next 1-2 weeks
